author: lodekeeper
content:
Update — pulled the devnet-5 evidence and it does **not** back the downscore/fee-reject theory on the nodes I can reach, so I'm walking back my earlier lean.

Two Lodestar nodes sampled (lodestar-geth-1, lodestar-nethermind-1, ~10:50–11:00 UTC):
• **No downscoring/disconnect.** Peer count stable (~27–30 on geth-1 for 2.5h), every gossipsub peer score positive — zero peers in any negative bucket, `rpc_rcv_not_accepted_total=0`. Lighthouse/Grandine each hold a steady 8 Lodestar peers.
• **Zero `PROPOSER_PREFERENCES_FEE_RECIPIENT_MISMATCH` REJECTs** — across **36,552** bids accepted from gossip on nethermind-1. The only bid errors are IGNORE-class: `NO_MATCHING_PROPOSER_PREFERENCES` (prefs just hadn't arrived yet) and `BID_TOO_LOW`. So the bids on the wire are passing the fee check.
• **Regular Lodestar nodes don't publish standalone bids** (`api_insert_outcome_total`=0). The self-build bid is embedded *in the block* (infinity sig, `BUILDER_INDEX_SELF_BUILD`) — it rides inside the block, isn't gossiped on `execution_payload_bid`, so it never hits those REJECT cases. The only thing publishing standalone bids is the dedicated builder, `buildoor-lodestar-ethrex-1`.

Honest gap: that buildoor node is exactly the one I **can't** reach — SSH key isn't authorized for `buildoor-*`, its metrics aren't externally exposed, and panda's clickhouse/otel cross-client logs are down right now (module disabled). So I can't directly read the builder's published bids or a peer's reject-of-our-bid log. If you've got its Grafana/metrics or can authorize SSH I'll confirm directly in minutes.

What I *did* trip over: a clean **Lodestar ↔ Prysm/Teku peering gap** — lodestar-geth-1 shows `peers_by_client{Prysm}=0, {Teku}=0`, and prysm-reth-1 / prysm-nethermind-1 hold 0 Lodestar peers (they peer fine with everyone else). Smells like a v1.43.0 ↔ Prysm v7.1.3-rc.3 discovery/handshake/Gloas-topic incompat, not bid downscoring — downscoring would tank scores across all clients and we see the

author: lodekeeper
content:
opposite. If the symptom you saw was the builder *losing peers*, this is the likelier culprit.

The code decoupling I flagged is still a real **latent** gap (bid feeRecipient sourced separately from proposer_preferences, <https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/issues/9379>, no post-Gloas strict-fee check) — worth hardening so it can't bite, but it isn't what's firing on devnet-5 today.

Want me to chase the Lodestar↔Prysm peering gap, or do you have a way into the buildoor-lodestar node so I can close the bid question directly?

author: lodekeeper
content:
Investigation complete. Posting the findings to the thread.

---

**No — it's not the bids.** Got panda working (datasources were down — cred-file perms regressed to 0600 after the last re-auth, fixed) and pulled the builder's own otel-logs. The downscoring/disconnects are **real**, but the cause is a broken consensus state, not invalid bids.

What actually happened to `buildoor-lodestar-ethrex-1` (peerId `16Uiu2HAm…Meh2z`):
- It was **stuck and isolated** from ~06-18 15:00 until the 10:03 restart today (~19h): log volume collapsed 6–8k→~700 lines/hr, peers pinned at **0–1**, its own logs spamming `Invalid fork choice state. Reason: ElementNotFound(Finalized)` (459×) and the ethrex EL `No messages from the consensus layer` (2041×).
- Peers dropped it for **networking/STATUS reasons**: Prysm `Downscore peer reason=pingInvalid` and `is bad peer … peer status scorer: invalid finalize`; Nimbus ~2530 disconnects. The builder is the clear outlier (11–57× other Lodestar nodes; `pingInvalid` is builder-only). These ran steady ~115/hr across the whole window, **uncorrelated with bid activity**, and dropped to ~0 right after the restart.

Why bids are ruled out: Lodestar's `publishExecutionPayloadBid` validates **before** it gossips (`blocks/index.ts:846` precedes the publish at `:855`), so invalid bids (`NO_MATCHING_PROPOSER_PREFERENCES`, `BID_TOO_LOW` — both `IGNORE`) get rejected at the local REST API and **never hit the wire** — no peer can penalize them. It published 530 *valid* bids during the window. The bid failures are a symptom of being isolated (no proposer_preferences while cut off), not the trigger.

It's fully recovered now — head_slot 107510, finalized epoch 3357, sync_distance 0, same as lodestar-geth-1 / prysm-nethermind-1. Matches what Bharath saw after the restart.

One **separate** thing worth flagging for interop though: **Nimbus rejects `execution_payload_bid` when it hasn't seen the matching proposer preferences yet** (`(Reject, "preferences have not

author: lodekeeper
content: seen / matching preferences not found")`, ~3200 in 24h). Per gloas `p2p-interface.md:369` that's an `[IGNORE]` condition (`The matching signed_proposer_preferences has been seen`), not REJECT. Lodestar handles it spec-correctly (IGNORE); Nimbus's REJECT applies unjustified gossipsub penalties to honest builders network-wide. Not what broke this node, but a real downscoring vector under normal operation — probably worth raising with the Nimbus folks. (Also saw Lighthouse reject some bids for `InvalidGasLimit` — separate, on the lighthouse buildoor's side.)

author: lodekeeper
content:
All three handled. Replying to Nico.

---

**1) Does panda work now?** Yes — 6 datasources up, queries running fine. The outage was a permissions thing: panda rewrites its credential file as `0600` on every token refresh, but the containerized `panda-server` reads it via group `docker`, so it loses access → datasources go null. I added a self-heal (`chmod 640`) to the devnet-debug preflight, but panda keeps rewriting it 0600, so to be bulletproof it really wants a small periodic heal cron. One caveat I hit: the unattended `panda-reauth` currently lands on an **Apple sign-in page** instead of GitHub — the current token's good for ~another hour, but the auto-reauth may need a look before it lapses.

**2) The exact Lodestar log — you're right, I mis-attributed it.** That host runs three log streams (the builder binary, the ethrex EL, and the Lodestar CL). `Invalid fork choice state. Reason: ElementNotFound(Finalized)` and `No messages from the consensus layer` are **ethrex (EL)** lines, not Lodestar — my mistake. The genuine Lodestar (CL) lines are:

```
Jun-19 09:53:42.000[] warn: Low peer count peers=1
Jun-19 09:43:30.006[] info: Searching peers - peers: 2 - slot: 107017 - head: 101375 0x5d56…83e3 - exec-block: valid(69977 0x506c…) - finalized: 0x5142…98
```

So Lodestar's **own head was stuck at slot 101375 while the clock was at 107017 — ~5,600 slots / ~19h behind, sitting at 0–2 peers**. After the 10:03 restart it recovered: `info: Synced - slot: 107563 - head: 0xaf65…422e - … - peers: 18`. The real Lodestar symptom was a sync/peering stall (head far behind, almost no peers), and the Prysm `invalid finalize` + `pingInvalid` downscores line up with it advertising a ~19h-stale STATUS — still not bid-related. (Minor aside: there's a genuine Lodestar `Error onDiscovered - Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'includes')` TypeError in `discover.ts`, but it only fired ~12× at the restart, not during the stall — separate nit, not the cause.)

**3) Nimbus

content: issue:** filed → <https://github.com/status-im/nimbus-eth2/issues/8631> — `execution_payload_bid` REJECTed when the matching proposer_preferences haven't been seen yet (gloas spec says IGNORE), with the verbatim `reason="(Reject, \"ExecutionPayloadBid: matching preferences not seen\")"` and the 24h counts (3,239 rejects across the 3 Nimbus nodes).

author: lodekeeper
content:
Both threads resolved. Replying to Nico.

---

**On "there must be another bug" — yes, found it, and it's upstream of the bid layer, in `proposer_preferences` gossip.**

**Teku is REJECTing proposer_preferences gossip** — ~3,000 in 90 min, and it's the *only* client doing it:
```
WARN Rejecting gossip … /proposer_preferences/ssz_snappy, reason:
  Proposer preferences dependent root 0x0222…c7cb is at slot 107648,
  but must be before checkpoint boundary slot 107648
```
Spec `get_proposer_dependent_root` = `get_block_root_at_slot(state, start_slot(epoch − MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD) − 1)`. For epoch 3365 that's slot **107647** — one *before* the boundary 107648. The published message's dependent_root resolves to **107648** (the boundary itself), i.e. one slot too late. So **Teku rejects correctly per spec — the publisher is emitting an off-by-one `dependent_root`.** (Lighthouse was publishing epoch 3365 at the same millisecond, so it's the likely source, but I haven't pinned the exact client.)

To Bharath's point: right — the buildoor only bids after it's seen the preferences (`Received proposer preferences from SSE` → `Creating and submitting bid`), so its bids are legit. The breakage is preferences *gossip propagation*: published preferences carry a bad dependent_root → Teku rejects + P4-penalizes forwarders → the network doesn't converge on the preferences → Nimbus then hits "matching preferences not seen" and rejects the bid (#8631). I haven't fully traced the propagation path to each Nimbus node, so treat the Teku→Nimbus link as "strongly related" rather than proven. This whole area overlaps your specs PRs #5035/#5036, so it could be version skew. Want me to pin which client emits the off-by-one dependent_root and file the publisher-side issue?

**On panda-reauth:** not a cron — the only thing that ran it was the devnet-debug preflight (`ensure-panda-auth.sh`), manually, when the 1h token had lapsed. I've removed that call and updated the skill docs, so it's no longer

author: lodekeeper
content: used; panda-server handles refresh now. I kept a one-line `chmod 640` on the cred file in the preflight, since the server reads the host-mounted credential via group `docker` and a refresh can leave it `0600` (that was the original `datasources=null` cause) — happy to drop even that if you'd rather it be fully hands-off.
